paper_title: Asymptotic structure. VI. Distant paths across a disc
summary: Conjecture 1.2 (the strong Coarse Menger conjecture for surfaces) remains open but has partial progress. Blažej, Pilipczuk, and Protopapas (arXiv:2605.11112, 2026) prove the weak form for graphs embeddable on any fixed surface: if fewer than k pairwise d-apart S-T paths exist, then there is a vertex set X with |X| ≤ f(k,d) such that every S-T path is within distance d of some vertex of X. This weak form allows |X| to grow with d, whereas Conjecture 1.2 requires the stronger bound |X| ≤ k independent of the distance parameter; the full conjecture remains open.

since_posted.title: A coarse Menger's Theorem for planar and bounded genus graphs
since_posted.authors: Václav Blažej, Michał Pilipczuk, Evangelos Protopapas
since_posted.claim: Proves the weak Coarse Menger conjecture for graphs embeddable on any fixed surface (Theorem 1.1): if fewer than k pairwise d-apart S-T paths exist, then there is a vertex set X with |X| ≤ f(k,d) such that every S-T path lies within distance d of X; the stronger bound |X| ≤ k required by Conjecture 1.2 is not established.

internal_refs_verified.note: False positive: this paper (Dujmović, Joret, Micek, Morin) proves an Erdős–Pósa-type result for pairwise far-apart cycles in general graphs, not the Coarse Menger conjecture for surfaces. The attributed conjecture (Chudnovsky–Seymour; Ahn–Gollin–Huynh–Kwon) and the result do not correspond to Conjecture 1.2 from 2509.07174.

search_queries: Coarse Menger conjecture surfaces Nguyen Scott Seymour 2509.07174 distant paths
arXiv:2605.11112 coarse Menger planar bounded genus graphs
notes: The weak Coarse Menger conjecture for surfaces is settled by arXiv:2605.11112 (2026), which cites Nguyen–Scott–Seymour's counterexample paper (part IV) but not part VI (2509.07174), likely because 2605.11112 was largely written before part VI appeared. The strong form of Conjecture 1.2 (∣X∣ ≤ k with ℓ depending only on k and c) remains open. The internal reference 2412.13893 is a fuzz-match false positive.
